req.title: Bulk data export
req.statement: A provisioned client shall request a FHIR bulk export; large datasets shall be paginated with NDJSON output.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-016 mandates three distinct test vectors: (1) a provisioned, authorised bulk-client successfully queues a FHIR $export job and receives an NDJSON stream (EP positive path); (2) an unauthorised caller receives HTTP 403 (NEG); (3) a dataset exactly at the pagination boundary exercises the paging contract and NDJSON line count (BVA). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that supply auth tokens and a pre-seeded FHIR server. State transition from 'accepted' → 'completed' is also verified in the positive case, covering STC implicitly. Decision-table thinking informs the 403 check: the single condition 'client authorised' fully determines the outcome.

tests.name: test_bulk_export_authorised_client_queues_job_and_produces_ndjson
preconditions: fhir-lite server is running and reachable
Client holds a valid bulk-data OAuth2 token with system/*.read scope
At least one Patient resource exists in the server
actions: POST (kick-off) GET /$export with Accept: application/fhir+json and Prefer: respond-async headers
Capture the Content-Location URL from the 202 response
Poll the status endpoint until HTTP 200 (complete) or timeout
Fetch each output URL listed in the completed manifest
Verify each fetched file is valid NDJSON
expected: Kick-off returns HTTP 202 with Content-Location header
Status endpoint eventually returns HTTP 200 with JSON manifest containing 'output' array
Each output file returns HTTP 200 with Content-Type application/ndjson
Every line of each output file is valid JSON
tests.executable:
import pytest
import time
import json

def test_bulk_export_authorised_client_queues_job_and_produces_ndjson(
    authorised_bulk_client,  # <fixture> returns requests.Session with valid token
    fhir_base_url,           # <fixture> returns server base URL string
    seed_patient_resources,  # <fixture> ensures >=1 Patient exists, yields count
):
    headers = {
        "Accept": "application/fhir+json",
        "Prefer": "respond-async",
    }
    kick = authorised_bulk_client.get(f"{fhir_base_url}/$export", headers=headers)
    assert kick.status_code == 202, f"Expected 202, got {kick.status_code}"
    status_url = kick.headers.get("Content-Location")
    assert status_url, "Content-Location header missing"

    manifest = <poll_until_complete>(authorised_bulk_client, status_url, timeout=30)
    assert manifest["output"], "Manifest output array is empty"

    for entry in manifest["output"]:
        resp = authorised_bulk_client.get(entry["url"])
        assert resp.status_code == 200
        assert "ndjson" in resp.headers.get("Content-Type", "")
        for line in resp.text.strip().splitlines():
            json.loads(line)  # raises if invalid JSON

tests.name: test_bulk_export_unauthorised_client_receives_403
preconditions: fhir-lite server is running and reachable
Caller has NO valid bulk-data token (uses invalid/expired credential)
actions: Attempt GET /$export with Accept: application/fhir+json and Prefer: respond-async headers but no or invalid Authorization token
Inspect the HTTP response status code and body
expected: Response body is a FHIR OperationOutcome with severity 'error'
tests.executable:
import pytest
import json

def test_bulk_export_unauthorised_client_receives_403(
    unauthorised_client,  # <fixture> returns requests.Session with bad/no token
    fhir_base_url,        # <fixture> returns server base URL string
):
    headers = {
        "Accept": "application/fhir+json",
        "Prefer": "respond-async",
    }
    resp = unauthorised_client.get(f"{fhir_base_url}/$export", headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}"

    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any(i.get("severity") == "error" for i in issues), (
        "OperationOutcome must contain at least one error-severity issue"
    )

tests.name: test_bulk_export_dataset_at_page_boundary_produces_correct_ndjson_line_count
preconditions: fhir-lite server is running and reachable
Client holds a valid bulk-data OAuth2 token
Server is seeded with exactly PAGE_SIZE Patient resources (boundary = last record of first page)
actions: Seed exactly PAGE_SIZE Patient resources to hit the exact page boundary
Collect all NDJSON output lines across all paginated files
Count total lines and compare against PAGE_SIZE
expected: Manifest output array has at least one entry (pagination boundary may yield 1 or 2 files)
Total NDJSON lines across all output files equals exactly PAGE_SIZE
trace: R-HLT-016: large datasets shall be paginated with NDJSON output
tests.executable:
import pytest
import json

PAGE_SIZE = <get_server_page_size>()  # <helper_fn> reads config or probes server

def test_bulk_export_dataset_at_page_boundary_produces_correct_ndjson_line_count(
    authorised_bulk_client,           # <fixture>
    fhir_base_url,                    # <fixture>
    seed_exact_patients,              # <fixture> factory; seeds exactly N patients
):
    seed_exact_patients(PAGE_SIZE)    # idempotent: wipes then seeds

    headers = {"Accept": "application/fhir+json", "Prefer": "respond-async"}
    kick = authorised_bulk_client.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/$export",
        params={"_type": "Patient"},
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert kick.status_code == 202
    status_url = kick.headers["Content-Location"]

    manifest = <poll_until_complete>(authorised_bulk_client, status_url, timeout=60)
    assert manifest["output"], "Expected at least one output entry"

    all_ids = []
    for entry in manifest["output"]:
        r = authorised_bulk_client.get(entry["url"])
        assert r.status_code == 200
        for line in r.text.strip().splitlines():
            rec = json.loads(line)
            all_ids.append(rec["id"])

    assert len(all_ids) == PAGE_SIZE, (
        f"Expected {PAGE_SIZE} records, got {len(all_ids)}"
    )
    assert len(all_ids) == len(set(all_ids)), "Duplicate records detected across pages"

tests.name: test_bulk_export_job_transitions_from_accepted_to_completed
preconditions: fhir-lite server is running and reachable
Client holds a valid bulk-data token
At least one resource of any type exists
actions: Send kick-off GET /$export; assert status 202 (state: ACCEPTED)
Immediately query Content-Location; assert status 202 or 200 (state: IN-PROGRESS or COMPLETE)
Verify no further poll after 200 returns 404 or error (terminal state)
expected: Intermediate polls: HTTP 202 with X-Progress header — state IN-PROGRESS
Final poll: HTTP 200 with manifest — state COMPLETE
State does not regress from COMPLETE back to IN-PROGRESS
